text: The Meridian Library opened the North Reading Room in 1912 after a two-year renovation. Archivist Lena Ortiz designed the catalog system for the new wing. The building stores city maps in cedar cabinets.

The Harbor Museum opened the North Painting Room in 1914 after a private fundraising drive. Curator Milo Chen arranged the first watercolor exhibit. The museum keeps shipping ledgers in a climate-controlled vault.

The Valley Observatory opened the North Telescope Room in 1916 after engineers reinforced the dome. Astronomer Priya Shah calibrated the primary lens. The observatory records comet sketches in leather notebooks.

The Prairie Archive opened the North Manuscript Room in 1918 after donors supplied fireproof shelving. Historian Omar Reyes translated the earliest field diary. The archive preserves settlement letters in linen folders.

The Summit Aquarium opened the North Coral Room in 1920 after biologists stabilized the saltwater tanks. Marine keeper Hana Brooks documented the first reef spawning. The aquarium stores tide charts beside the pump station.

The Cedar Theater opened the North Rehearsal Room in 1922 after carpenters rebuilt the balcony. Director Anika Patel coached the inaugural radio drama. The theater keeps costume sketches in oak drawers.
